The Grafton.editorialSummary: This laid-back Irish bar offers pub grub, pints & plenty of TVs for catching the game.

Standings.editorialSummary: Pennant-lined walls, TVs & craft beers keep sports fans in the rooting spirit at this tiny bar.

International Bar.editorialSummary: Small, spare East barroom drawing locals who like their drinking with no pretensions.

Coyote Ugly Saloon.editorialSummary: Outpost of a festive saloon chain with female bartenders, bartop dancing & body shots.

Niagara Bar.editorialSummary: This longtime watering hole on a busy Alphabet City corner features DJ-spun tunes & a photo booth.

Lucy's Bar.editorialSummary: Easygoing hangout, famed for its eponymous owner, features pool, video games & a laid-back vibe.

Doc Holliday's.editorialSummary: Texas roadhouse-style bar in the East Village with biker bartenders & country music on the jukebox.

Cienfuegos.editorialSummary: Fancy rum cocktails are paired with Latin small plates at this second-floor East Village lounge.

The Library.editorialSummary: Longtime barroom with cheap drinks, crowd-pleasing jukebox & B movies projected on the walls.

Death & Co.editorialSummary: Bartenders in bow ties & suspenders recall the speakeasy era at this dark, moody cocktail lounge.

The Summit Bar.editorialSummary: Crystal chandeliers & wood/steel benches contribute to a sophisticated scene at this cocktail bar.

Alphabet City Beer Co.editorialSummary: Cozy bar/beer store with tap selections, growler fills, a cheese & meat counter & bottles to go.

KGB Bar.editorialSummary: Readings & publication parties are regular events at this tiny Soviet-themed watering hole.

McSorley's Old Ale House.editorialSummary: Old-time bar with Irish bartenders, 2 kinds of ale (light or dark) & sawdust on the floor.

Beauty Bar.editorialSummary: Nightclub chain featuring martinis, DJs & dancing in a retro-salon setting. Most offer manicures.

Please Don't Tell.editorialSummary: Patrons who enter through a phone booth in Crif Dogs next door sip novel cocktails in a dark space.

The Wayland.editorialSummary: Cutting-edge cocktails are served in a no-frills setting at this laid-back barroom.

d.b.a..editorialSummary: A smartly curated selection of beer, bourbon & beyond draws connoisseurs to this East Village bar.

Juke Bar.editorialSummary: Lively, Southern-flavored joint with signature cocktails, craft beers & a live band on Sundays.

Holiday Cocktail Lounge.editorialSummary: Once a classic dive, this completely redone space now has a swanky look, food & big-time drinks.

Ten Degrees.editorialSummary: Petite St. Marks Place wine bar offering a variety of vinos paired with small plates.

The Headless Widow.editorialSummary: Creative cocktails & craft beers pair with elevated bar bites like oysters at this stylish hangout.

Bua.editorialSummary: East Villagers down discounted cocktails on a lively patio during this pub's daytime happy hour.

Solas.editorialSummary: Busy bar on first floor with a dark, smaller lounge above, where a DJ keeps the dancing going.

Goodnight Sonny.editorialSummary: By the Wayland team, this tile-floored space offers a raw bar, sandwiches & seasonal cocktails.

Lucky.editorialSummary: Small, easygoing bar & community event space featuring seasonal cocktails & a backyard beer garden.

Mona's.editorialSummary: Relaxed, brick-walled bar with a pool table, Skee-Ball & a juke box, plus frequent live music.

The Spotted Owl Tavern.editorialSummary: Convivial hangout with vintage decor offering eclectic bar fare, cocktails & beers on tap.

Berlin.editorialSummary: Tiny, dimly-lit entertainment venue featuring DJs & live bands along with cocktails & beer.

Club Cumming.editorialSummary: Buzzy, celeb-owned bar with drag & cabaret shows, plus other diverse entertainment.

Big Bar.editorialSummary: Intimate bar & gathering place featuring a red lighting scheme & a relaxed atmosphere.

Wiggle Room.editorialSummary: Beer, wine & inventive cocktails fixed up in a buzzy bar with live DJs & an edgy vibe.

Double Down Saloon.editorialSummary: NYC offshoot of a longtime Las Vegas punk bar, best known for its bacon martinis.

Sake Bar Decibel.editorialSummary: Underground sake bar offering a large list of varietals along with Japanese snacks.

Barcade.editorialSummary: A spin-off of a Williamsburg bar, this hang has vintage video games (Pac-Man, Donkey Kong) & beer.

Blue & Gold Tavern.editorialSummary: Veteran East Village watering hole offering cheap drinks & pool in a bare-bones atmosphere.

Otto's Shrunken Head.editorialSummary: Rock bands, a Polynesian-esque setting & cocktails enliven this offbeat tiki bar.
